name: EU/EEA qualification review seed pack
change_note: Initial regional seed pack with routing requirements only; member-state and official EU sources must be checked per case.

regulator_map.notes: Verify current product safety, market surveillance, responsible person, and conformity route.
Verify food law, novel food, additives, claims, labeling, and member-state obligations.
Verify responsible person, safety assessment, notification, ingredient, and claims obligations.
Verify EU trademark status, owner, class, and territory when brand rights affect the decision.

checklist_hints: Check whether an EU responsible person, importer, economic operator, EORI/VAT/IOSS route, or member-state language requirement applies.
For CE-marked or regulated goods, verify conformity assessment, declaration, technical file, labeling, and authorized representative route.
Member-state rules can add language, notification, recycling, or category constraints; verify the exact destination country.

requirements.requirement: Determine whether the EU route needs an importer, responsible person, authorized representative, or other economic operator.
requirements.decision_effect: Cannot approve regulated EU product routes until the required EU economic operator path is verified.
requirements.notes: Seed routing requirement; verify the exact category and member state.

requirements.requirement: For regulated goods, verify conformity assessment, label language, warnings, CE/other marks, and claims against current EU and member-state sources.
requirements.decision_effect: Missing conformity basis or label obligations requires more information or human escalation.
requirements.notes: Do not infer CE eligibility from a logo alone.

sources.title: European Commission: General Product Safety Regulation
sources.confirms: EU product safety rules can require responsible economic operators and product safety information for consumer products.

sources.title: European Commission: Market surveillance and product compliance
sources.confirms: EU market surveillance rules cover product compliance, economic operators, and enforcement for goods in the single market.

sources.title: European Commission: Economic Operators Registration and Identification number
sources.confirms: EORI is used to identify economic operators in customs procedures in the EU.

sources.title: European Commission: CE marking
sources.confirms: CE marking indicates that a product has been assessed to meet EU safety, health, and environmental protection requirements where applicable.

sources.title: European Commission: Safety Gate
sources.confirms: EU Safety Gate provides official alerts for dangerous non-food products and supports recall/safety screening.

gaps: Add current EU official sources for GPSR/market surveillance, food, cosmetics, chemicals, electronics, and batteries.
Add member-state examples before marking validated.
Add golden cases covering CE route, responsible person missing, and prohibited/restricted product outcomes.
